notes_md:
**dạ** = a polite, respectful “yes.” You use it when speaking to someone older, higher status, or to show good manners.

It often stands alone as a soft, obedient-sounding reply, or comes at the beginning of a sentence to show respect.  

It doesn’t add new meaning to the sentence — it adds politeness and humility. Compared to **vâng** (also “yes”), **dạ** feels softer and more deferential, especially toward parents, teachers, or elders.
exampleSentencesTranslated: Yes (respectful), I have already finished my homework.
When mom calls, I usually reply immediately with a yes (respectful).
Sister, yes (respectful), can I help you bring things into the kitchen?
exampleSentences: Dạ, em đã hoàn thành bài tập về nhà rồi ạ.
Khi mẹ gọi, anh thường trả lời ngay bằng một tiếng dạ.
Chị ơi, dạ em có thể giúp chị mang đồ vào bếp không?

definitions.vi: Từ dùng để đáp lại một cách lễ phép khi được người lớn gọi hoặc hỏi.
definitions.en: A polite word used to respond respectfully when addressed by elders or superiors.

example.en: When my mom called, I immediately replied: "Yes!"

notes:

It doesn’t add new meaning to the sentence — it adds politeness and humility. Compared to vâng (also “yes”), dạ feels softer and more deferential, especially toward parents, teachers, or elders.
